Event: He has a crush on Jackie for most of the show
Explanation: Persistent unreciprocated crush makes him lonely without clear benefit to her.

Event: Jackie finally returns his feelings in the final season
Explanation: Mutual romance late in the story benefits both emotionally.

Event: He constantly fawns over Kelso
Explanation: Expressing affection strengthens their close friendship.

Event: He once has a sex dream about Kelso and reacts to it
Explanation: Private fantasy slightly boosts his own confidence without affecting others.

Event: Fez talks to Hyde so engagingly Hyde feels “half in love with him”
Explanation: His attention makes Hyde feel special and affirms Fez’s social charm.

Event: He adopts and uses the nickname “Fez” as his given name
Explanation: Simplifying his identity helps him function socially with no evident harm.

Event: He names his business “Chez Fez”
Explanation: Branding a business on his nickname supports his success and entertains others.

Event: He is nearly always seen snacking on junk food
Explanation: Enjoyment is offset by implied weight gain and health/fitness downsides.

Event: He pretends not to be a virgin early on
Explanation: Saves face slightly but reinforces his insecurity without changing their views.

Event: He becomes more successful with women in the final season
Explanation: He achieves his long‑term romantic goal while giving partners desired attention.

Event: He makes a tape of the circle’s ramblings
Explanation: Creates a shared artifact of their time together with minor mutual benefit.

Event: He calls Kelso stupid on that tape
Explanation: Teasing slightly lifts his own status at some social cost to Kelso.

Event: Later he rushes to Kelso’s defense when others call Kelso stupid
Explanation: Loyal defense strengthens their bond and moderates group cruelty.

Event: He obsessively pursues women as a “Casanova wannabe”
Explanation: He occasionally gains dates while subjecting women to unwanted pursuit.

Event: He openly indicates he’ll “be in his bunk” when turned on
Explanation: He relieves his frustration but makes friends uncomfortable, seen with Donna.

Event: He becomes more comfortable making sexual remarks to friends
Explanation: His increased openness gratifies him but squicks and annoys others.

Event: He confronts Caroline about her hostility to his friends
Explanation: Protects his friends’ comfort and asserts boundaries in the relationship.

Event: He breaks up with Caroline over her hostility
Explanation: Sacrificing a relationship improves his and his friends’ well‑being.

Event: He stalks Jackie by spying on her through a window for years
Explanation: His voyeurism gratifies him while gravely invading her privacy and safety.

Event: He stalks Donna similarly
Explanation: Same invasive spying yields him secret pleasure while seriously harming her interests.

Event: He tells Jackie he’s been spying on her for three and a half years
Explanation: Confession risks his relationships and reputation while deeply disturbing her.

Event: He hides in closets to spy on women or couples
Explanation: His covert watching serves his arousal while violating others’ privacy and trust.

Event: He repeatedly makes unwanted moves on women
Explanation: Occasional success is outweighed by distress and discomfort caused to women.

Event: He becomes “even more creepy and disgusting” toward friends over time
Explanation: Escalating behavior undermines his friendships while offering only fleeting thrills.

Event: He expresses relief his sister is far away from Kelso
Explanation: Protective distancing may shield his sister and slightly reassures Fez.

Event: He tells his sister would be “off‑limits” to Kelso
Explanation: Clear boundary protects his sister and clarifies expectations with Kelso.

Event: He and Kelso look for an apartment together
Explanation: Co‑housing solves their living need and strengthens their partnership.

Event: Fez applies for a lease from Fenton despite being his enemy
Explanation: He pursues a good apartment while dragging a personal nemesis into the situation.

Event: He now hates Kelso in That ’90s Show for Jackie leaving him
Explanation: Nursing resentment harms his own happiness and damages a deep friendship.

Event: In the sequel, he commits to one woman instead of serial perving
Explanation: Stable commitment improves his life and gives partner security and respect.

Event: He possibly makes up contradictory facts about his home country to mess with friends
Explanation: Teasing entertains him but mildly misleads and frustrates his friends.
